items.title: Carly Rae Jepsen Announces New Double Album Day And Night
items.summary: Carly Rae Jepsen, queen of critically acclaimed cult-favorite pop stars, has recently been teasing a return, and it's about time. She's back in a few months with a big new double album called Day And Night. The post Carly Rae Jepsen Announces New Double Album <em>Day And Night</em> appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Clive Davis Dead At 94
items.summary: Clive Davis, one of the most successful and iconic executives in the history of the record business, has passed away. Over decades in the business, Davis pushed the careers of stars like Barry Manilow, Whitney Houston, and Alicia Keys, as well as signing dozens of others. The New York Times reports that Davis passed away at home in Manhattan today, after a recent hospitalization for respiratory problems.

items.title: London Hardcore Stompers Dynamite Share New Three-Song Promo
items.summary: Dynamite haven't released a full-length yet, but they've already played all over the world and perfected their driven, purposeful, straight-ahead style. Dynamite collaborated with people like High Vis' Graham Sayle and Turnstile's Meg Mills on their 2023 debut EP Blow The Bloody Doors Off!, and they followed it with last year's Settle The Score. The post London Hardcore Stompers Dynamite Share New Three-Song Promo appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: The B-52s and Devo announce 2026 ‘Cosmic De-Evolution’ tour
items.summary: Devo also have dates with Billy Idol in August.

items.title: Clive Davis, music industry legend, dead at 94
items.summary: “Most people don’t realize how much the music they love was shaped by one man,” Barack Obama said of Clive Davis.

items.title: Team Dresch announce first album in 30 years: hear “One Song”
items.summary: ‘Furthermore’ is Pacific Northwest queercore greats Team Dresch’s first album since 1996 and will be out September 26.

items.title: Goose Honor Fan Who Died in Fall at New York Concert
Richie Faulkner Names Band That Influenced Song About 'Rupture'
Legendary Music Exec Clive Davis Dies At 94
items.summary: Clive Davis, the legendary music executive whose career touched virtually every corner of popular music, died today (June 22) at his Manhattan home. He was 94.

items.title: Goose Salutes Fan Who Died During MSG Show
items.summary: Less than 24 hours after a fan died during Goose’s concert at New York's Madison Square Garden, the band honored him with a moment of silence and a night dedicated to healing.

items.title: Mind the Gap
items.summary: If music can be a time machine, the Instagram account @gapplaylists is a DeLorean. Exactly what it sounds like, Gap Playlists shares the monthly in-store music playlists from Gap, from the early 1990s through today. Each post activates your sense memory, taking you back to the inside of a mall in, say, September 1998.

items.title: Twin Atlantic announce emotional, honest new album ‘Separation From The Animals’ with rousing single ‘In Your Eyes’ and 2026 tour

That baggage is basically what the whole band is about" The post Twin Atlantic announce emotional, honest new album ‘Separation From The Animals’ with rousing single ‘In Your Eyes’ and 2026 tour appeared first on NME.
